title: tephrosia pumila subsp. aldabrensis
intro: Tephrosia pumila subsp. aldabrensis, also known as Aldabra milk bush or Aldabra shrub, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Tephrosia pumila subsp. aldabrensis include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and alleviating symptoms of various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, have been used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief to those in need.
